title: Resolve remaining UX open questions in user-flows.md
description: Six open UX questions remain in docs/requirements/user-flows.md that need user input: (1) Shopping list lifecycle — when is a list done? (2) Calendar integration surface — in-app or push to Google Calendar? (3) AI Suggest button placement on Meal Plan Timeline. (4) Generate Shopping List trigger location — Meal Plan tab, Shopping tab, or both? (5) Meal Plan history browsing — scroll up in timeline or separate view? (6) Default launch tab for returning users.

title: Update rating model from like/dislike to 5-star across requirements files
description: The user-flows.md document specifies a 5-star rating system (average + count displayed top-right of Recipe Detail title row), replacing the original like/dislike model. Requirements files (identity.md, recipes.md, meal-planning.md, history.md) still reference the old like/dislike model and need to be updated to reflect the 5-star decision.
close_reason: Fixed in upcoming PR — updated identity.md, meal-planning.md, recipes.md, ai-features.md, decisions.md, project.md

title: Review data model for snapshot revisions
description: Review how recipe snapshots and revisions work in the data model. Key question: clarify when we reference the recipe (current/live version) vs. when we need a revision (frozen snapshot). Specifically: (1) Meal plan entries reference a recipe — do they always use a snapshot, or link to the live recipe with a snapshot captured at plan time? (2) Shopping list items are generated from meal plan ingredients — do they reference the live recipe, the meal plan entry, or the snapshot? (3) Should revision tracking be more structured (version numbers, revision history table) vs. simple point-in-time copies? (4) What happens to meal plan snapshots when a recipe is edited — does the snapshot stay frozen while the recipe list shows the updated version? The goal is a clear data model for recipe → revision → meal plan → shopping list relationships.
